task: Phase 11 v0.1.8 — 5 deferred Phase 10 tradeoffs (Option B scope split). In-session: US-1101 codegen install.ps1 + US-1102 keychain.ts 6-line format-parity (closes #1) + US-1105 Linux Docker smoke. Deferred doc-only: US-1103 Win VM checklist + US-1104 Authenticode procurement runbook. Plan: .omc/plans/phase-11-deferred-tradeoffs-v2.md (ralplan round 2 APPROVE).

stories.title: scripts/codegen-install-version.ts — extend to also sync bin/install.ps1 $ReleaseVersion
acceptanceCriteria: scripts/codegen-install-version.ts — add 4th syncFile() invocation against bin/install.ps1, mirror pattern of existing 3 (install.sh, index.ts, telemetry.ts).

Regex must preserve PowerShell single-quote literals around version: pattern roughly `/^(\$ReleaseVersion = if \(\$env:AXHUB_PLUGIN_RELEASE\) \{ \$env:AXHUB_PLUGIN_RELEASE \} else \{ ')v\d+\.\d+\.\d+(?:-[a-z0-9.]+)?(' \})$/m`
Captured groups (1) and (2) preserve surrounding context including single-quote pair.

tests/codegen-version.test.ts — add 2 NEW test cases: (a) syncInstallVersion() also writes bin/install.ps1 with new version, (b) pre-release tag '0.1.8-rc.1' handling round-trips correctly through both install.sh and install.ps1.
bun test → 372 pass / 5 skip / 0 fail (370 baseline + 2 new).
bunx tsc --noEmit → clean.
MUST land BEFORE US-1102 (D1 — codegen test mutates bin/install.ps1 in tests; if US-1102 lands first the new ps1 sync regex isn't there yet, codegen test throws at line 42 missing-expected-version).

stories.title: keychain.ts — 6 existing one-line errors → 4-part empathy template (closes GitHub issue #1)
acceptanceCriteria: Rewrite 6 keychain.ts Korean error strings to 4-part format (감정 / 원인 / 해결 / 다음액션) per error-empathy-catalog.md and Phase 9 keychain-windows.ts precedent. Lines: 57, 62, 79, 85, 90, 95.

Line 65 (catch [System.Net.WebException] error path) — UNREACHABLE per Bun.spawnSync ENOENT semantics (returns exitCode:null, doesn't throw). Add NOTE comment instead of rewriting: '// NOTE: Bun.spawnSync returns exitCode:null on ENOENT, so this catch is unreachable in practice. Kept for type-safety. Format-parity skipped (D2).'

Each rewritten error must (a) preserve original semantic kernel (e.g., line 79 must still mention 'libsecret-tools'), (b) have 4 parts separated by \n, (c) start with 감정 word ('잠깐만요', '아이고', '죄송해요' etc.).

tests/keychain.test.ts — extend with 6 NEW test cases (1 per rewritten line). Each asserts: (1) message contains 감정 prefix, (2) message contains 원인:, 해결:, 다음:, (3) message preserves semantic kernel keyword.
bun test → 378 pass / 5 skip / 0 fail (372 baseline post-US-1101 + 6 new).

Issue #1 close protocol: precheck `gh issue view 1 --json state,locked,closed`, capture to .omc/evidence/issue-1-close-attempt.txt; if state=OPEN AND locked=false: `gh issue close 1 --comment '<commit URL> — 6 lines fixed, 1 deferred per D2 unreachable catch'`; if locked or already closed, surface to commit body, no silent skip.
Blocked by: US-1101

stories.title: tests/smoke-linux-docker.sh — Linux secret-tool keychain bridge runtime smoke harness
acceptanceCriteria: tests/smoke-linux-docker.sh (NEW) — bash script that builds + runs ubuntu:24.04 container with libsecret-tools, secret-service backend (libsecret + dbus-x11 shim), runs `secret-tool store --label=axhub service axhub` to seed test token, then runs axhub-helpers-linux-amd64 token-init inside container.

tests/smoke-linux-docker.Dockerfile (NEW) — base image PINNED to explicit sha256 digest: `FROM ubuntu:24.04@sha256:<digest>` where digest captured via `docker manifest inspect ubuntu:24.04 | jq -r '.manifests[0].digest'` AT TIME OF WRITING. Add comment with capture command + date for reproducibility.
If user lacks docker: script gracefully exits 0 with message 'docker not available — skipping Linux smoke (run on machine with docker for v0.1.8 evidence)'. NOT a failure mode.

Evidence file .omc/evidence/phase-11-linux-docker-smoke.txt — first line MUST be exact LIMITATION banner: 'LIMITATION: libsecret-tools CLI path only — Coverage: 40% READ-path (axhub-helpers contract). End-to-end ~15% (ax-hub-cli WRITE-path D-Bus is upstream). Does NOT validate gnome-keyring-daemon, kwalletd5, or headless systemd-keyring user-bus.'
Banner appears in 3 places: (1) Dockerfile header comment, (2) shell script header, (3) evidence artifact first line.
If docker available + smoke passes: evidence file follows banner with: timestamp + container sha256 + secret-tool exit code 0 + axhub-helpers token-init exit code 0 + extracted token first 16 chars.
If docker available + smoke fails: evidence file follows banner with FAILED + exit codes + docker logs tail.

stories.title: docs/pilot/windows-vm-smoke-checklist.md + tests/smoke-windows-vm-checklist.ps1 — companion runbook + executor
acceptanceCriteria: docs/pilot/windows-vm-smoke-checklist.md (NEW) — 14-step manual checklist for next pilot session: (1) provision Win11 VM (Parallels/UTM/Azure free tier), (2) install Claude Code 2.1.84+, (3) `/plugin install axhub@axhub`, (4) confirm SessionStart fires session-start.ps1, (5) confirm install.ps1 auto-downloads windows-amd64.exe, (6) verify cmdkey /list:axhub shows credential post-axhub-auth-login, (7) run axhub-helpers token-init, (8) verify ~/.config/axhub-plugin/token (mode 0600 equivalent on NTFS), (9) PreToolUse + PostToolUse hooks fire on bash commands, (10) ExecutionPolicy Restricted fallback test, (11) AMSI/EDR detection test (skip if no AV available), (12) MAX_PATH test (Korean profile + deep root), (13) NTLM proxy test (skip if no proxy), (14) capture full transcript to docs/pilot/v0.1.x-windows-vm-result.txt.

tests/smoke-windows-vm-checklist.ps1 (NEW) — companion PowerShell executor. First line: `if ($env:AXHUB_VM_SMOKE -ne '1') { exit 0 }`. Each of 14 steps becomes a function with try/catch + $LASTEXITCODE check + ConvertFrom-Json validation for hook envelope shape. Pester optional (bare try/catch sufficient).
Both files must NOT block any current build/test (companion .ps1 only runs when AXHUB_VM_SMOKE=1).
tests/manifest.test.ts (extend) — assert checklist file exists + has 14 numbered steps + companion .ps1 exists with $env:AXHUB_VM_SMOKE guard.

stories.title: docs/pilot/authenticode-signing-runbook.md + .github/workflows/sign-windows.yml.template + signing stub
acceptanceCriteria: docs/pilot/authenticode-signing-runbook.md (NEW) — vendor procurement steps: (a) Sectigo OV cert ($300+/yr), (b) HSM/KMS for signing key (Azure Key Vault / AWS KMS), (c) GitHub Actions windows-latest runner config with cert secret, (d) Authenticode signtool command + cosign integration retention.

.github/workflows/sign-windows.yml.template (NEW) — workflow_dispatch-only YAML template, NOT triggered automatically. Steps: (1) checkout, (2) download windows-amd64.exe artifact, (3) `signtool verify /pa <file>` with continue-on-error: true (unsigned binary exits non-zero), (4) emit telemetry through EXISTING src/axhub-helpers/telemetry.ts channel: event 'windows.signing.skipped' with reason 'vendor_cert_pending'.
.gitattributes (NEW or extend) — add line: `*.yml.template linguist-language=YAML linguist-detectable=false` so GitHub doesn't classify as workflow file + linters skip.
If .eslintignore exists in repo: add `*.yml.template` line. If not exist: skip this step (don't create just for one entry).

When AXHUB_SIGNING_STUB=1 env var set in CI: stub workflow becomes active. Until vendor procurement completes, telemetry shows 'signing stubbed' allowing release pipeline to acknowledge unsigned-binary state without breaking.
Runbook explicitly states: 'This is paperwork + executable scaffold. Day procurement clears, integration is one PR (s/AXHUB_SIGNING_STUB=1// + s/continue-on-error: true// + add cert secret config).'
tests/manifest.test.ts (extend) — assert runbook exists, .yml.template exists with workflow_dispatch on header, .gitattributes line present.
